headline: Hulu's 'Devil in Silver': Dan Stevens Plunges into Psychological Horror Maze
lede: Acclaimed British actor Dan Stevens tackles mental healthcare's darkest corridors in Hulu's gripping new psychological thriller. The six-part series promises a harrowing exploration of institutional nightmares.
body:
Hulu is set to redefine psychological horror with its adaptation of Victor LaValle's searing novel *The Terror: Devil in Silver*. Showrunners Chris Cantwell and LaValle have crafted a narrative that transcends traditional genre boundaries, transforming a psychiatric hospital into a metaphorical battleground of human vulnerability and systemic dysfunction.

Stevens plays a new patient thrust into a dilapidated medical facility where the line between institutional reality and supernatural terror blurs dramatically. The series promises a nuanced examination of mental health infrastructure, weaponizing horror tropes to expose deeper societal wounds.

Production details reveal meticulous attention to the source material's complex psychological landscape. The adaptation leverages LaValle's intricate storytelling, which simultaneously critiques American healthcare systems and explores mythological undercurrents of institutional trauma.

Early festival screenings suggest a breakthrough performance from Stevens, who continues to demonstrate remarkable range after critically acclaimed turns in *Legion* and *Downton Abbey*. The actor's ability to navigate psychological complexity appears perfectly matched to the series' ambitious narrative architecture.
